description.task: Create a Product class where the constructor parameters have the same names as the instance fields. Use the 'this' keyword to resolve the ambiguity.
instructions: Inside the constructor, use this.name = name; this.price = price; this.quantity = quantity;
Do NOT rename the constructor parameters.
Do NOT change display() or main().
description.hint: When a local variable (or parameter) shadows an instance field with the same name, 'this.fieldName' explicitly refers to the instance field.
description.expectedOutput:
Product: Laptop | Price: $999.99 | Qty: 5
Product: Mouse | Price: $29.99 | Qty: 20

description.task: Create a Circle class with two constructors. Use this() in the second constructor to delegate to the first, avoiding code duplication.
description.context: this() calls another constructor in the same class. It must be the first statement in a constructor and avoids repeating logic across multiple constructors.
instructions: Complete the secondary constructor using this(radius, "White") to delegate to the primary.
Do NOT change the primary constructor or main().
Both constructors should print the same message format — the secondary achieves this through delegation.
description.hint: this(radius, "White") must be the very first line in the secondary constructor. No other code can come before it.
description.expectedOutput:
Circle created: radius=5.0, color=Red
Circle created: radius=3.0, color=White

description.task: Implement a Builder-style class where setter methods return 'this', allowing method calls to be chained in a single expression.
description.context: Returning 'this' from a method enables a fluent API / builder pattern, making code more readable — e.g., new User().setName("Alice").setAge(25).build().
instructions: Implement setTable(), setCondition(), and setLimit() — each assigns the value and returns 'this'.
Return type of each setter must be QueryBuilder.
Do NOT change build() or main().
description.hint: Return type: public QueryBuilder setTable(String table) { this.table = table; return this; }
description.expectedOutput: SELECT * FROM users WHERE age > 18 LIMIT 50
